description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a modern, rectilinear design, featuring a grid of large windows on all sides that allow for ample natural light. The facade alternates between dark and light panels, with the lighter sections appearing as concrete or stucco and the darker sections as metal or glass. The building has multiple floors, each with a consistent window pattern, and balconies or terraces are visible on one side, likely for employee use. The ground floor includes large entryways, possibly for both pedestrian and vehicular access, and the roof appears to have mechanical equipment and open areas. The structure is adjacent to a separate, lower rectangular building, possibly a parking garage or service annex. The overall appearance is sleek and functional, typical of contemporary urban office architecture.
description: A modern, multi-story office building with a grid of large windows, alternating light and dark facade panels, and balconies on one side. The structure is adjacent to a lower annex, likely a garage or service building.
description_view_0: This view shows the side with a dark facade and a grid of large windows, as well as the adjacent lower annex building.
description_view_1: This view highlights the light-colored facade with a regular window pattern and visible balconies on the right side.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entrance area, a symmetrical window grid, and the full width of the building with the annex to the left.
description_view_3: This view shows the opposite dark facade, with the balconies on the left and the building's upper structure visible.
attribute_window_pattern: Regularly spaced, large rectangular windows arranged in a grid pattern across all facades, with consistent vertical and horizontal alignment.
